exercises.exerciseTypeName: A: Write one word in each gap
exercises.title: A: Write one word in each gap
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.passage:
Dear Sophie,
Hi! How are you? I went shopping with my cousin yesterday and tried (1) ...... several jackets. I was really surprised (2) ...... how many different styles there were. It reminded me (3) ...... the afternoon we spent in that huge shopping centre last summer.
I found a coat that is very much (4) ...... fashion right now, but I couldn't do it (5) ...... because the zip was broken! I might go back (6) ...... the weekend to see if they have another one. I bought some boots, though – they're similar (7) ...... the black ones you bought last month. I've got them (8) ...... now and they're really comfortable.
Also, I changed my old dress (9) ...... a skirt. I cut the top part (10) ...... and it looks much better!
Write soon,
Emily

exercises.exerciseTypeName: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
exercises.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
exercises.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
'I want to visit the art gallery,' Liam said. **said**
Liam ...... to visit the art gallery.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is want, so the correct answer is "said he wanted".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "said he wanted", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "said he wanted" is a phrasal verb or fixed chunk that collocates with the rest of the sentence.

items.sentence:
'We're designing a new museum,' said Mrs Parker. **they**
Mrs Parker ...... a new museum.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is present continuous, so the correct answer is "said they were designing".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "said they were designing", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence:
'We're meeting the architect tomorrow,' Daniel said. **day**
Daniel said they were meeting the architect ...... .

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is tomorrow, so the correct answer is "the following day".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "the following day", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence:
'Will you help me paint this wall?' Lucy asked. **paint**
Lucy asked ...... this wall.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is request, so the correct answer is "me to help her paint".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "me to help her paint", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

exercises.title: E: Complete the sentences
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: The architect prepared the ...... for the new library.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is designs, so the correct answer is "plans".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: Builders are ...... a new school near the park.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is construct, so the correct answer is "building".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: The old wall was ...... from local stone.

explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

explanationContent.grammarFocus: Relative clauses add detail about a noun — choose the pronoun that matches the antecedent.
